description: A designed pull-quote graphic styled after a news-outlet quote card. A black header bar at top carries the white 'Blaze media' logo (with its flame-stylized 'B'). Below, on a cream/off-white background, a large rust-red sans-serif quotation fills the frame: "'By rooting out fraud and abuse, the Department of Labor and our federal partners will ensure that highly skilled jobs go to Americans first.'" The minimalist design relies solely on bold typography and the Blaze Media branding for credibility, with no photo or illustration. The propaganda technique borrows a third-party media outlet's framing to lend external validation to the administration's 'Project Firewall' H-1B crackdown, emphasizing 'Americans first' for skilled jobs. It targets a general/working audience receptive to immigration-restriction and pro-American-worker messaging.
notable_text: 'By rooting out fraud and abuse, the Department of Labor and our federal partners will ensure that highly skilled jobs go to Americans first.'
